objective: Close the remaining GAP-009 reviewer-artifact loophole by requiring review-state/v1 reviewer artifact receipts to be pass, current, claim_support, non-empty, and cross-bound to path, expected producer, and PR head before they can validate.

linearSnapshot.scopeNote: Linear remains planning and ownership context only for this slice. This intent does not use Linear as implementation, CI, review, or closeout proof.

currentFinding.reviewStateValidator: Reviewer artifact receipts already require review_artifact kind, review-state ref prefix, exact path ref, expected producer match, PR head match, and positive size for pass receipts.
currentFinding.remainingLoophole: A reviewer artifact receipt can currently be fail, blocked, unknown, stale, or orientation-only and still satisfy review-state/v1 validation unless another closeout layer rejects it later.
currentFinding.desiredInvariant: A review-state reviewer artifact is claim-supporting only when the packet-level validator itself rejects non-pass, non-current, non-claim-support, or empty artifact receipts.

intentReviewFindingsAddressed.finding: Strict packet rejection can collapse stale or non-claim-supporting reviewer artifact evidence into generic invalid-packet failures.
intentReviewFindingsAddressed.response: The implementation must preserve role-addressable error paths in review-state validation and add Judge/PM regression evidence that downstream blocker semantics remain explicit for stale and non-claim-supporting reviewer artifacts.

intentReviewFindingsAddressed.finding: The test matrix under-specified not_applicable and missing freshness branches.
intentReviewFindingsAddressed.response: The validation plan now requires explicit not_applicable status and missing/not_applicable freshness cases with exact error-path assertions.

intentReviewFindingsAddressed.finding: Schema discoverability may drift from runtime invariants.
intentReviewFindingsAddressed.response: The schema must encode representable invariants with supported JSON Schema keywords, while path/ref, producer, and head-SHA cross-field rules remain TypeScript runtime validator proof.

assumptions: review-state/v1 reviewer artifacts are intended to support closeout/Judge coverage, not merely orient future work.

If a reviewer artifact exists only as blocked, stale, unknown, or orientation-only evidence, the review-state packet should fail validation or force an explicit blocker path rather than silently validating as coverage.
The Judge/PM audit already rejects unsupported reviewer artifacts; this slice moves the same trust boundary into the review-state packet validator and regression tests.
The schema can become stricter for reviewerArtifacts.receipt without changing the top-level evidence-receipt/v1 schema.
This slice should not refresh live GitHub, CodeRabbit, or Linear state.
nonGoals: Do not add a new PR closeout command.
Do not change external-state, runtime-card, delivery-truth verdict semantics, or harness next behavior.
Do not fetch live PR comments, CI checks, CodeRabbit state, or Linear issue data.
Do not rewrite the review-state data model beyond reviewer artifact receipt support rules.
Do not claim GAP-009 fully closed unless focused tests, schema parity, required skill lenses, and independent reviewers pass or are explicitly blocked with evidence.
implementationApproach: Add review-state validator checks that every reviewer artifact receipt has status pass, freshness current, evidenceUse claim_support, and sizeBytes greater than zero.
Keep existing path, producer, headSha, kind, and ref binding behavior intact.
Add regression tests for fail, blocked, unknown, and not_applicable reviewer artifact receipt statuses with exact reviewerArtifacts.<index>.receipt.status error paths.
Add regression tests for stale, missing, unknown, and not_applicable reviewer artifact receipt freshness with exact reviewerArtifacts.<index>.receipt.freshness error paths.
Add regression tests for orientation and audit_trail reviewer artifact evidenceUse values with exact reviewerArtifacts.<index>.receipt.evidenceUse error paths.

Add or confirm Judge/PM audit regression coverage for stale and non-claim-supporting reviewer artifacts so downstream closeout still surfaces reviewer-specific blockers rather than a generic invalid-packet collapse.

Tighten contracts/review-state.schema.json so reviewerArtifacts.receipt advertises representable status, freshness, evidenceUse, kind, and positive size requirements with validator-supported JSON Schema keywords.

Keep cross-field invariants that JSON Schema cannot express here, such as receipt.ref matching artifact.path, receipt.producer matching expectedProducer, and receipt.headSha matching pr.headSha, in TypeScript validator tests.
Run schema/example parity and focused test gates before recording the slice receipt.
validationGates: pnpm exec biome check src/lib/review-state/validation.ts src/lib/review-state/review-state.test.ts src/lib/delivery-truth/judge-pm-audit.test.ts contracts/review-state.schema.json contracts/examples/review-state.example.json .harness/intent/codex-runtime-evidence-verifier-cockpit-pu-023-gap-009-reviewer-artifact-claim-support-intent.json
riskControls: Fail closed for reviewer artifacts that are stale, blocked, unknown, fail, not_applicable, orientation-only, or audit-trail-only.
Do not reinterpret failed or blocked reviewer artifacts as missing; preserve the exact validation error path for operator action.
Keep review-state validator changes additive to evidence-receipt/v1 and do not narrow unrelated receipt kinds.
Schema parity is shape and discoverability proof only; claim-support safety is proven by review-state validator tests plus Judge/PM audit tests.
If schema validator cannot express a TypeScript cross-field rule, keep that rule in TypeScript tests and document the schema limitation in the receipt rather than pretending parity proves it.
Unavailable required skill lenses must be recorded as blocked_validation with owner and exact missing-skill evidence, not substituted with prose.
acceptanceCriteria: review-state/v1 rejects reviewer artifact receipts whose status is fail, blocked, unknown, or not_applicable.
review-state/v1 rejects reviewer artifact receipts whose freshness is stale, missing, unknown, or not_applicable.
review-state/v1 rejects reviewer artifact receipts whose evidenceUse is orientation or audit_trail.
review-state/v1 rejects reviewer artifact receipts without positive sizeBytes regardless of status.
Existing path, expectedProducer, headSha, ref, kind, and ownership-classification tests still pass.
review-state public schema/example parity remains valid.
Review-state validation tests assert exact error paths for status, freshness, evidenceUse, and sizeBytes failures so agents can route remediation without parsing prose.
Judge/PM audit tests prove stale and non-claim-supporting reviewer artifacts still produce reviewer-artifact-specific blockers.
Slice receipt records GAP-009, Project Brain inputs, memory surfaces, validation commands, skill-lens results, and independent reviewer results.
blockedConditions: Stop if tightening review-state would break a documented requirement that reviewer artifacts may be orientation-only inside review-state/v1.
Stop if the required schema parity validator cannot run and no equivalent focused proof is available.
Stop if independent reviewers report that this belongs in delivery-truth only rather than review-state validation.
rollbackPath: Revert the review-state validator additions and their tests.
Revert review-state schema constraints if they are too strict for packet admission.
Keep the intent and review artifacts as evidence of the attempted GAP-009 closure and record the blocker in receipts.jsonl.
